variants.title: Lightning makes air explode into thunder
variants.source_license: Derived analytics brief; new media required before render
variants.description: Watch the lightning flash behind this animal behavior: Auroras glow when solar particles hit air
variants.seo_title: Lightning makes air explode into thunder
variants.yt_description: Lightning makes air explode into thunder. Watch the lightning flash first, then replay the opening clue.
variants.hook: Lightning turns air into a shock wave.
variants.script: Lightning turns air into a shock wave. Watch the flash first, because lightning heats a narrow path of air extremely fast. That air expands suddenly, and the pressure wave reaches us as thunder. The sound is the atmosphere snapping back. Which storm signal next?
variants.lead: Lightning turns air into a shock wave. Watch the flash first, because lightning heats a narrow path of air extremely fast. That air expands suddenly, and the pressure wave reaches us as thunder. The sound is the atmosphere snapping back. Which storm signal next?

variants.title: Snakes smell the air with their tongues
variants.source_license: Derived analytics brief; new media required before render
variants.description: Watch the tongue smell behind this animal behavior: Snakes smell the air with their tongues
variants.seo_title: Snakes smell the air with their tongues
variants.yt_description: Snakes smell the air with their tongues. Watch the tongue smell first, then replay the opening clue.
variants.hook: Snakes sample the air with a tongue flick.
variants.script: Snakes sample the air with a tongue flick. Watch the tongue flick, because it collects scent particles and sends them to a special organ in the mouth. That helps snakes track prey, mates, and danger without needing a loud chase. The air is information. Would you notice the trail?
variants.lead: Snakes sample the air with a tongue flick. Watch the tongue flick, because it collects scent particles and sends them to a special organ in the mouth. That helps snakes track prey, mates, and danger without needing a loud chase. The air is information. Would you notice the trail?

variants.title: Cats groom to reset their scent map
variants.source_license: Derived analytics brief; new media required before render
variants.description: Watch the grooming signal behind this animal behavior: Cats use whiskers like built-in rulers
variants.seo_title: Cats groom to reset their scent map
variants.yt_description: Cats groom to reset their scent map. Watch the grooming signal first, then replay the opening clue.
variants.hook: Cats use grooming to reset their scent map.
variants.script: Cats use grooming to reset their scent map. Watch the tongue and paw movements, because grooming spreads familiar scent across the coat. variants.title: Plants use trap hairs to count touches before snapping shut
variants.source_license: Derived analytics brief; new media required before render
variants.description: Watch the trap hairs behind this animal behavior: Plants use trap hairs to count touches before snapping shut
variants.seo_title: Plants use trap hairs to count touches before snapping shut
variants.yt_description: Plants use trap hairs to count touches before snapping shut. Watch the trap hairs first, then replay the opening clue.
variants.hook: Plants use trap hairs to count touches before closing.
variants.script: Plants use trap hairs to count touches before closing. Watch the trap hairs, because the plant waits for more than one touch before spending energy on a snap. That delay helps it ignore rain and debris, then close when prey is real. Which plant trick should we decode next?

variants.title: Dogs read the world through scent maps
variants.source_license: Derived analytics brief; new media required before render
variants.description: Watch the scent map behind this animal behavior: Dogs build a map with smell
variants.seo_title: Dogs read the world through scent maps
variants.yt_description: Dogs read the world through scent maps. Watch the scent map first, then replay the opening clue.
variants.hook: Dogs build a map with smell.
variants.script: Dogs build a map with smell. Watch the nose before the feet, because every sniff samples layers of scent. Dogs can learn who passed, where they moved, and what changed since the last check. The ground is basically a news feed. What should they sniff next?

variants.title: Butterflies carry color on tiny wing scales
variants.source_license: Derived analytics brief; new media required before render
variants.description: Watch the wing scales behind this animal behavior: Butterflies are covered in tiny color scales
variants.seo_title: Butterflies carry color on tiny wing scales
variants.yt_description: Butterflies carry color on tiny wing scales. Watch the wing scales first, then replay the opening clue.
variants.hook: Butterflies are covered in tiny color scales.
variants.script: Butterflies are covered in tiny color scales. Watch the wing surface, because butterfly color often comes from thousands of tiny overlapping scales. Some scales use pigment, and others bend light itself. That is why one wing can look painted up close. Which tiny detail should we zoom into next?
